title: lippia praecox var. nyikensis
intro: Lippia praecox var. nyikensis, also known as Uvukile or Mountain bush tea, is a plant species native to the mountainous regions of East Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Lippia praecox var. nyikensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
